Social Content Calendar

30-day pre-launch social media content calendar with posts for Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, and Reddit

Social Content Calendar

This document contains the complete 30-day pre-launch social media content calendar for HeyZack, organized by week and platform.

Voice Guidelines: See Brand Voice & Tone for messaging calibration by audience.

Campaign Overview

ElementDetails
TimelineT-30 days to Launch Day
Content Mix40% Educational, 30% Social Proof, 20% Product Features, 10% Entertainment
Voice StrategyB2B: Professional/Data-driven
Primary GoalBuild anticipation and thought leadership without being overly promotional

Week 1: Problem Awareness & Empathy (Days 1-7)

Day 1

Twitter/X (B2C) — 8:00 AM EST

Post:

“You have 7 different apps just to control your home. Ring for cameras. Nest for thermostat. Philips for lights. August for locks. SmartThings hub that stopped working after an update.

There has to be a better way. (There is.)”

ElementDetails
Hashtags#SmartHome #HomeAutomation #TechFrustration
VisualSplit-screen: phone with 7+ apps vs. single unified app
EngagementAsk: “How many smart home apps do YOU have?”
TypeEntertainment (Relatable frustration)

LinkedIn (B2B) — 7:30 AM EST

Post:

“Property managers: You’re paying $85K per building for traditional BMS systems. Then $15K/year for ‘maintenance’ that’s really just vendor lock-in.

Every thermostat adjustment requires a service call. Every service call costs $200/hour. Your ownership is asking: ‘Can we scale without bleeding cash?’

What if I told you there’s a 95% cost reduction solution that actually gives you MORE control?

Thread below 👇“

ElementDetails
Hashtags#PropertyManagement #BuildingAutomation #PropTech
VisualCost comparison infographic: Traditional BMS ($85K) vs. Modern Solution ($5K)
EngagementMulti-post thread with case study data; encourage DMs for whitepaper
TypeEducational (Cost awareness)

Day 2

Instagram (B2C) — 9:00 PM EST

Post:

“POV: You’re lying in bed at 11 PM wondering if you locked the front door.

Again.

For the 3rd time this week.

Your ‘smart’ lock requires opening an app, waiting 8 seconds for it to connect, hoping it actually shows you real-time status…

What if your home just TOLD you? ‘Front door locked. Back door locked. Garage closed. Goodnight.’

That’s what actual intelligence looks like. Coming soon. 👀“

ElementDetails
Hashtags#SmartHome #HomeSecurity #PeaceOfMind
VisualCozy bedroom scene with phone notification overlay
EngagementStory poll: “Do you check your locks at night?” Yes/No
TypeProduct Feature (teaser)

Day 3

Twitter/X (B2B) — 10:00 AM EST

Post:

“You manage 30 apartment buildings. Tenant in Building F calls: ‘It’s too hot.’

Traditional BMS: Schedule $200 service call, wait 2 days, tenant leaves 1-star review.

AI-powered control: Open dashboard, see that unit’s thermostat remotely, adjust in 90 seconds, text tenant confirmation.

Resolution time: 90 seconds vs. 2 days.”

ElementDetails
Hashtags#PropertyTech #Multifamily #TenantExperience
VisualBefore/after flowchart of problem resolution
EngagementAsk property managers: “What’s your average HVAC complaint resolution time?”
TypeSocial Proof (use case scenario)

Day 4

TikTok/Reels (B2C) — 7:00 PM EST

Post:

“Things that make you go 🤬

[Scene 1] Smart home updates break automations - again
[Scene 2] ‘Alexa, turn off bedroom lights’ - ‘I found several devices named bedroom…’
[Scene 3] 2 AM false alarm because cat walked by camera
[Scene 4] Trying to explain your smart home to visiting parents

Tag someone who FEELS this 😭“

ElementDetails
Hashtags#SmartHomeFail #TechProblems #Relatable
VisualQuick-cut comedy format with exaggerated reactions
EngagementDuet/stitch invitation; use trending comedy audio
TypeEntertainment

Day 5

LinkedIn (B2B) — 11:00 AM EST

Post:

“CASE STUDY: Hotel operator cuts energy costs by $11K annually across 12 rooms.

The setup:

  • 12-room boutique hotel, 75% occupancy
  • Old system: HVAC runs 24/7 in every room

The fix:

  • Smart occupancy detection via door locks + motion sensors
  • Automatic climate adjustment: Guest departs → HVAC to eco mode

The math:

  • 5 hours × 12 rooms × 275 days/year × $0.35/kWh = $11,482 saved
  • System cost: $3,600 | Payback: 4.7 months

Want the full breakdown? Comment ‘DATA’ and I’ll DM you the case study.”

ElementDetails
Hashtags#HospitalityTech #HotelManagement #EnergyEfficiency
VisualClean data visualization showing monthly savings curve
EngagementLead magnet (case study PDF) via DM
TypeEducational (Case study)

Day 6

Instagram (B2C) — 12:00 PM EST

Post:

“My electricity bill used to make me cry. $240/month.

Then I got curious: WHERE is all that money going?

Found the culprits: 🔴 Basement beer fridge running 24/7 (old seal = energy vampire) 🔴 Kids’ bathroom lights left on… for 11 hours… daily 🔴 Heating empty bedrooms while we’re all downstairs

Smart energy monitoring showed me EXACTLY where the waste was.

Three months later: $164/month. That’s $912/year saved.

Anyone else have energy vampires hiding in their house? 👇“

ElementDetails
Hashtags#EnergySavings #SmartHome #SustainableLiving
VisualBefore/after energy dashboard showing consumption by room
EngagementAsk followers to share their biggest energy wasters
TypeSocial Proof (testimonial style)

Day 7

Reddit (r/homeautomation) — 6:00 PM EST

Title: “After 6 years and $4K+ in failed smart home attempts, I finally figured out what was wrong”

Body:

Like many of you, I started in 2018 with a Nest thermostat and thought ‘This is the future!’

Then came:

  • SmartThings hub (buggy, constant disconnects)
  • Philips Hue ecosystem ($600 in bulbs)
  • Ring cameras (false alarm hell)
  • IFTTT for automations (broke every 3 months)

I was managing 9 different apps. My wife refused to use any of it.

Then I realized: The problem wasn’t the individual products. It was the fragmented ecosystem.

What I wish I knew then:

  1. Multi-protocol support matters more than brand loyalty
  2. Local control > cloud-only
  3. AI learning > rule-based scheduling
  4. One app controlling everything > ‘works with Alexa’

What lessons did YOUR smart home journey teach you?

ElementDetails
VisualNone (text post for authenticity)
EngagementEngage authentically in comments; share journey, not product
TypeSocial Proof (Community storytelling)

Week 2: Solution Introduction & Education (Days 8-14)

Day 8

Twitter/X (B2C) — 9:00 AM EST

Post:

“‘AI smart home’ usually means: ✗ If-then rules you program manually ✗ Timers that break when your schedule changes ✗ Geofencing that triggers when you’re still 3 blocks away

ACTUAL AI smart home means: • Learns ‘Fridays they stay up late’ without programming • Adjusts climate 15 min before you arrive (not 15 min after) • Knows ‘Tuesday delivery guy is normal, Sunday stranger isn’t’

There’s a difference.”

ElementDetails
Hashtags#AI #SmartHome #MachineLearning
VisualSide-by-side comparison chart: Rule-Based vs. AI Learning
EngagementAsk: “Has your ‘AI’ smart home ever learned something unexpected?”
TypeEducational

Day 10

Instagram Carousel (B2C) — 11:00 AM EST

Post:

“Behind the scenes: How AI actually learns your home 🧠

Week 1: Observing • Watches when lights turn on/off • Tracks when you adjust thermostat • Notes when doors lock/unlock

Week 2-3: Pattern recognition • ‘They wake up 6:45 AM weekdays, 8:30 AM weekends’ • ‘Living room lights on 7 PM-11 PM consistently’

Week 4+: Proactive automation • Starts warming house at 6:30 AM weekdays (15 min early) • Detects: ‘Window left open while AC running - alert sent’

What would YOU want your home to learn first? 👇“

ElementDetails
Hashtags#SmartHome #AI #TechExplained
VisualAnimated graphic showing AI learning timeline
EngagementCarousel post (swipe through stages); ask for dream automation
TypeEducational

Day 11

Twitter/X Thread (B2B) — 10:00 AM EST

Thread:

🧵 THREAD: How to scale property management without scaling your team

Managing 10 buildings with a 3-person team? Here’s how operators are managing 40+ with the same headcount:

1/ Centralized dashboard
Stop driving to properties for thermostat adjustments. One screen shows every building’s climate, security, access control.

2/ Predictive maintenance
AI detects: ‘HVAC in Building M cycling 40% more than baseline.’ Schedule $800 preventive fix instead of $12K emergency replacement.

3/ Automated tenant experience
New tenant moves in → Access codes auto-generate and expire → Climate pre-conditions unit → Zero manual steps.

4/ Portfolio energy optimization
AI learns occupancy patterns across all properties. Average savings: 28% on utilities.

5/ Remote problem resolution
Tenant: ‘Heat isn’t working.’ Check remotely: thermostat set to 62°F. Adjust to 72°F, text tenant: ‘Fixed.’ Resolution: 2 minutes.

6/ Data-driven owner meetings
Show ownership: ‘Q3 energy costs down $47K across portfolio. Tenant complaints down 65%.’

Want the full portfolio scaling playbook? DM me ‘SCALE‘“

ElementDetails
Hashtags#PropertyManagement #PropTech #RealEstate
EngagementLead magnet (playbook PDF) for DM requests
TypeEducational

Day 12

TikTok/Reels (B2C) — 8:00 PM EST

Post:

“Showing my parents my smart home was a MISTAKE 😂

Dad: ‘Alexa, turn off lights’
Alexa: ‘I found 3 devices named lights. Which one?‘
Dad: ‘THE LIGHTS’
Alexa: ‘I’m sorry, I don’t—‘
Dad: gets up and uses light switch

Me: ‘Wait Dad, try—‘
Mom: ‘Why is the thermostat talking to you?‘
Me: ‘It’s asking if I want to save energy by—‘
Mom: ‘Is it listening to us right now?‘
Me: ‘Well technically—‘
Mom: unplugs everything

Lesson: Your smart home should be intuitive enough that your parents don’t think you’re in a sci-fi horror movie.

#SmartHome #ParentHumor #TechProblems”

ElementDetails
Hashtags#SmartHome #ParentHumor #TechFail #Relatable
VisualComedy skit format with text overlays
EngagementUse trending audio; encourage duets/stitches
TypeEntertainment

Day 14

Instagram (B2C) — 1:00 PM EST

Post:

“I saved $47/month without changing my lifestyle. Here’s how:

✗ Didn’t sacrifice comfort ✗ Didn’t nag family about lights ✗ Didn’t manually adjust thermostats all day

• Automated the energy police: • Rooms turn off lights after 5 min empty • Only heat/cool occupied spaces • Climate optimizes when we’re asleep

The result after 3 months: Before: $243/month average After: $196/month average Savings: $564/year

That’s a vacation fund. Or 140 fancy coffees. ☕

Drop a 💰 if you’re spending too much on utilities”

ElementDetails
Hashtags#EnergySavings #MoneySavingTips #HomeAutomation
VisualBefore/after bill screenshots + savings breakdown
EngagementStory poll: “Is your energy bill over $200/month?”
TypeSocial Proof

Week 3: Product Reveals & Demo (Days 15-21)

Day 15

Twitter/X (B2C) — 10:00 AM EST

Post:

“We built the smart home we always wanted but couldn’t buy.

• One app controls everything (not 7) • Actually learns habits (not just timers) • Works with devices you already own • Setup in 30 minutes (not 30 hours) • No monthly fees for core features

Name: HeyZack Launch: 15 days Website: heyzack.ai

RT if you’re tired of smart home disappointment.”

ElementDetails
Hashtags#SmartHome #HeyZack #ProductLaunch
VisualClean product hero shot + app interface screenshot
EngagementRT campaign; encourage sign-ups for launch notification
TypeProduct Feature

Day 16

LinkedIn (B2B) — 8:30 AM EST

Post:

“After 200+ conversations with property managers and hospitality operators, we built HeyZack:

Portfolio-scale building automation at 95% lower cost than traditional BMS.

What you told us you needed: ✓ Centralized dashboard (one platform, all properties) ✓ DIY installation (no $85K labor costs) ✓ Real-time ROI tracking (prove savings to ownership) ✓ No vendor lock-in (open protocols, data export tools)

What we delivered: • $5K per building vs. $85K traditional BMS • 20-30% energy cost reduction • One dashboard for unlimited properties • Deploy new property in 2 hours

Early access program opens next week. Limited to 50 operators.

Property managers: Want a preview? Comment ‘DEMO’ below.”

ElementDetails
Hashtags#PropertyManagement #PropTech #BuildingAutomation
VisualProfessional product dashboard screenshot with ROI data
EngagementEarly access waitlist via comments/DMs
TypeProduct Feature

Day 19

TikTok/Reels Demo (B2C) — 7:30 PM EST

Post:

“POV: Your smart home ACTUALLY works

[Scene 1: Morning] Person wakes up Lights gradually brighten automatically Text overlay: ‘Learned I wake up 6:45 AM weekdays’

[Scene 2: Leaving] Grabs keys Phone notification: ‘All doors locked. Climate set to eco mode. Security armed.’ Text: ‘One command, everything handled’

[Scene 3: At work] Checks phone ‘Package delivered at front door - here’s the video’ Text: ‘Real alerts, not false alarms’

[Scene 4: Coming home] 15 minutes from house Text: ‘Pre-heating to 70°F before arrival’

[Scene 5: Bedtime] ‘Goodnight’ All lights off, doors lock, security arms Text: ‘Finally. A smart home that just works.’

HeyZack. Launching next week.”

ElementDetails
Hashtags#SmartHome #HeyZack #HomeAutomation #TechTok
VisualSmooth POV transitions, clean text overlays
EngagementUse trending ‘day in my life’ format; encourage saves
TypeProduct Feature (Demo)

Day 21

Reddit AMA Announcement — 12:00 PM EST

Title: “[ANNOUNCEMENT] We built an AI-powered smart home platform after $4K in failed attempts with existing solutions. AMA this Friday 7 PM EST”

Body:

After 6 years and $4K+ trying to make smart home tech actually work reliably, my co-founder and I built HeyZack - the platform we wish existed in 2018.

What we focused on:

  • One app for everything (tired of 7+ apps)
  • Genuine AI learning (not just rule-based scheduling)
  • Multi-protocol support (WiFi, Zigbee, Matter)
  • Local + cloud hybrid (works during internet outages)
  • No subscription ransoming

We’re launching next week.

AMA this Friday, 7 PM EST. Ask me anything about architecture decisions, why we chose certain protocols, pricing philosophy, or just roast our decisions.

ElementDetails
EngagementCommunity-first approach; genuinely answer hard questions
TypeSocial Proof (Community engagement)

Week 4: Social Proof & Launch Countdown (Days 22-28)

Day 22

Instagram Stories Series (B2C)

Story 1: “Beta tester spotlight: Meet Sarah 👋“
Story 2: “Sarah’s problem: False alarms at 2 AM”
Story 3: “After 2 weeks with HeyZack: AI learned her delivery schedule”
Story 4: “‘I’ve slept through the night for 2 weeks straight.’ - Sarah” + Poll
Story 5: “7 days until launch. Early access link in bio 👆“

Day 25

TikTok/Reels (B2C) — 8:00 PM EST

Post:

“Things my smart home learned without me teaching it:

✗ Never programmed this • House figured it out

  1. ‘Friday nights they stay up until 1 AM’
  2. ‘They adjust thermostat to 75°F when cooking’
  3. ‘Mail arrives between 2-3 PM’ - stops alerting
  4. ‘They leave for work 8:15 AM on Tuesdays’
  5. ‘Window left open while AC running’ - alerts immediately

That’s actual AI. Not timers. Not rules I programmed.

6 days until launch. Link in bio.”

ElementDetails
Hashtags#SmartHome #AI #HeyZack #MachineLearning
VisualText overlay on home footage
EngagementEncourage ‘What would you want it to learn?’ comments
TypeProduct Feature

Day 26

Instagram (B2C) — 12:00 PM EST

Post:

“My 72-year-old mom set up HeyZack. By herself. In 41 minutes.

If she can do it, you can do it.

‘I just scanned the little square thing and followed the pictures. Easier than my old TV remote.’ - Mom

5 days until launch. We built this so EVERYONE can have a smart home, not just engineers.

Tag someone who ‘isn’t tech-savvy’ but deserves a smart home 👇“

ElementDetails
Hashtags#SmartHome #UserFriendly #TechForEveryone
VisualCandid photo/video of mom using app
EngagementEncourage tags for gift-giving angle
TypeSocial Proof

Day 28

LinkedIn (B2B) — 8:00 AM EST

Post:

“3 days until HeyZack launches.

Early access for property managers & hospitality operators is now OPEN (first 50 only).

What you get: ✓ Portfolio dashboard (manage unlimited properties) ✓ AI energy optimization (20-30% savings) ✓ Real-time ROI tracking ✓ Priority onboarding support

What it costs: • $2K-$8K per building (vs. $85K traditional BMS) • Zero service contracts • Zero vendor lock-in

What we need from you: • Deploy in at least one building within 30 days • Share results data (anonymized) for case studies • Give honest feedback

Application link: [heyzack.ai/early-access-b2b]“

ElementDetails
Hashtags#PropTech #PropertyManagement #EarlyAccess
EngagementEarly access application via link
TypeProduct Feature

Launch Week: Final Countdown (Days 29-30)

Day 29 — Twitter/X (B2C)

Post:

“Tomorrow.

After 3 years of development. 18 months of beta testing. 2,400+ testers across residential and commercial.

HeyZack goes live.

Early bird pricing: $799 (40% off retail) Limited to first 500 backers

9:00 AM PT. Kickstarter.

See you there.”

Day 30 — ALL PLATFORMS

Launch Day Post:

”→ WE’RE LIVE

HeyZack is on Kickstarter right now.

Early Bird: $799 (40% off retail) First 500 backers only

One app. No monthly fees. Actually learns your habits.

[LINK TO KICKSTARTER]

This is what smart homes should have been all along.”

Content Type Legend

TypeContent Category
EducationalInformative content that teaches concepts
Social ProofTestimonials, case studies, user stories
Product FeatureProduct demos and capabilities
EntertainmentEngaging, relatable content

Posting Time Reference (EST)

PlatformBest B2C TimesBest B2B Times
Twitter/X8-10 AM, 12-1 PM7-10 AM
LinkedIn—7-8 AM, 11 AM
Instagram11 AM-1 PM, 7-9 PM—
TikTok/Reels7-8 PM—
Reddit6-8 PM—

For visual asset specifications and engagement scripts, refer to the source document.
